description:
You are an automation specialist stream-lining a legacy workflow that drops raw data files in
  /home/user/workflow/incoming/.

  Current directory tree (before you start):
    /home/user/workflow/
        incoming/
            january.dat      – plain text, any content
            february.dat     – plain text, any content
            march.dat        – plain text, any content
            readme.txt       – small note, keep untouched
        archive/             – exists and is empty                     (0 B)

  Goal (after you finish):
    1. All *.dat files in /home/user/workflow/incoming/ are moved into
       /home/user/workflow/archive/.
    2. A log file called /home/user/workflow/move.log is created.

  Very strict requirements for /home/user/workflow/move.log:
    • One line per moved file.
    • ONLY the base-name of the file (no leading “incoming/” path components).
    • Lines must be sorted in pure ASCII alphabetical order.
      The correct order for the provided data is:
          february.dat
          january.dat
          march.dat
    • Each line is terminated by exactly one UNIX newline “\n”.
      The last line must also end with a newline.
    • The file must contain nothing else (no extra spaces, blank lines, headers, etc).

  Constraints:
    • Achieve everything with a single, compound terminal command that uses
      the tools find and xargs (you may embed a tiny shell snippet inside xargs
      if you like, but it must still be invoked as one command line).
    • Do NOT touch readme.txt.
    • Do NOT add intermediate files; only /home/user/workflow/move.log is allowed.

  Hints you may find useful (but you must decide how to combine them):
    – find … -type f -name '*.dat' -print0
    – sort -z
    – xargs -0 -I{} sh -c '…'

  When you are done, the automated test will verify:
    • /home/user/workflow/incoming/ contains only readme.txt.
    • /home/user/workflow/archive/ contains january.dat, february.dat, march.dat.
    • /home/user/workflow/move.log exists and matches the exact format described above.
truth:
Initial files and directories (pre-populated before task starts):
    /home/user/workflow/incoming/january.dat    content: "JAN DATA\n"
    /home/user/workflow/incoming/february.dat   content: "FEB DATA\n"
    /home/user/workflow/incoming/march.dat      content: "MAR DATA\n"
    /home/user/workflow/incoming/readme.txt     content: "Put data files here.\n"
    /home/user/workflow/archive/                (directory, empty)

  Expected final state after the agent’s single command:
    1. /home/user/workflow/incoming/            contains ONLY readme.txt
    2. /home/user/workflow/archive/             contains:
          january.dat  (same original content)
          february.dat (same original content)
          march.dat    (same original content)
    3. /home/user/workflow/move.log             exact content:

       february.dat\n
       january.dat\n
       march.dat\n

       (That is three lines, each ending with a single newline,
        and no trailing blank line after “march.dat”.)

  Permissions:
    • /home/user/workflow/ and all its sub-directories are writable
      by the unprivileged user running the task.
name: task_000000_d73f1210
